formations_blocks: Colombia pressing hard in the attacking phase
DR Congo defending deeper after an early attacking transition
DR Congo using quick transitions down the left channel
key_matchups: Jhon Arias vs DR Congo midfield
Díaz as the left-sided outlet in Colombia's attacks
phases.summary: The opening phase is defined by DR Congo’s willingness to break quickly down the left, but Colombia soon seize control through sustained pressure and territory. Colombia generate the clearer chances from wide service and second-ball pressure, while Mpasi is forced into key interventions to keep the game level.

formations_blocks: Colombia high press / immediate counterpress in midfield
adjustments: DR Congo slow the game down after surviving early pressure
Colombia lose some early fluidity and both teams begin breaking down before the final third
After the hydration break, momentum is interrupted and the game becomes more fragmented
phases.summary: Colombia dominate the opening phase with sustained pressure, repeated shots from range and entries into the box, but DR Congo respond by sitting deep and relying on Lionel Mpasi’s shot-stopping. After the hydration break the rhythm drops, Colombia’s fluency fades, and both sides struggle to progress into the final third, with DR Congo briefly threatening through Wissa’s pressure on Vargas.

formations_blocks: DR Congo in a resolute defensive block in their own half
Colombia sustained final-third possession against a deep defence
Colombia’s attacking phase relied heavily on long-range shooting rather than penalty-area entries
adjustments: DR Congo made a half-time substitution: Noah Sadiki on for Ngal’ayel Mukau
key_matchups: Cédric Bakambu vs Colombia goalkeeper Vargas on the 43rd-minute cross
Colombia shooters vs DR Congo goalkeeper Lionel Mpasi, who saved six on-target efforts
phases.summary: Colombia dominated territory and circulation around DR Congo’s final third, but the visitors stayed compact and difficult to break down, limiting clear penalty-area touches. The game’s best openings came from distance for Colombia and a near-breakthrough header chance for Bakambu, while DR Congo’s only notable attacking threat before the interval was largely sporadic. At half-time the match remained goalless, with DR Congo still set up to absorb pressure and Colombia chasing a breakthrough.

formations_blocks: DR Congo defensive line holding firm in a compact defensive block
Colombia probing in sustained possession with side-to-side circulation
adjustments: DR Congo start the second half
Colombia make a double substitution on 59': Juan Quintero for James Rodríguez and Jhon Córdoba for Luis Suárez
DR Congo respond on 59': Simon Banza for Cédric Bakambu
Luis Díaz repeatedly moves inside from the left and attacks from central-left zones
Juan Quintero comes on as a deeper/central creator between the lines
key_matchups: Luis Díaz vs DR Congo defenders / Mpasi
Luis Suárez pressing the DR Congo defence
Jhon Arias vs DR Congo’s back line on the rebound chance
Juan Quintero linking play into Muñoz on the right side
phases.summary: The second half opens with Colombia dominating the ball but finding DR Congo difficult to break down, as the hosts sit in a compact defensive shape and clear repeated entries into the box. Colombia’s best moments come through Luis Díaz and a series of central combinations, but the breakthrough never arrives and both sides turn to attacking substitutions around the hour mark to change the game.

formations_blocks: Colombia pressing high and then managing the game after taking the lead
DR Congo resorting to direct play / long balls out of defence
DR Congo attacking in the final third with width late on
adjustments: Colombia’s goal comes from a through-ball and off-ball movement that opens space after Jhon Córdoba goes to ground
DR Congo introduce Charles Pickel and Nathanaël Mbuku, with Mbuku replacing Samuel Moutoussamy and Edo Kayembe going off at the hydration break
DR Congo shift toward more direct attacking and throw-in/set-piece pressure in the closing minutes
key_matchups: Lionel Mpasi vs Colombia’s distance shots / finishing attempts
Daniel Muñoz exploiting space behind the DR Congo defence
Colombia defence vs Aaron Wan-Bissaka’s throw-in delivery
phases.summary: Colombia broke the deadlock through a clever attacking sequence, with Quintero’s through-ball and Muñoz’s run punishing DR Congo’s defensive lapse. After the goal, Colombia kept pressing for a second while DR Congo grew more direct and increasingly threw bodies forward, but Mpasi’s saves and Colombia’s organization in the box kept the visitors in the match.
